Skip to contents
rpolars 0.2.0
  • Reference

Polars Expr

Source: R/expr__expr.R
Expr.Rd

Polars pl$Expr

Usage

Expr_lit(x)

Expr_suffix(suffix)

Expr_prefix(prefix)

Expr_reverse()

Arguments

x

an R Scalar, or R vector (via Series) into Expr

suffix

string suffix to be added to a name

prefix

string suffix to be added to a name

Value

Expr, literal of that value

Expr

Expr

Expr

Details

pl$lit(NULL) translates into a typeless polars Null

Examples

2+2
#> [1] 4
#Expr has the following methods/constructors
ls(rpolars:::Expr)
#>   [1] "abs"              "add"              "agg_groups"      
#>   [4] "alias"            "all"              "and"             
#>   [7] "any"              "append"           "apply"           
#>  [10] "arccos"           "arccosh"          "arcsin"          
#>  [13] "arcsinh"          "arctan"           "arctanh"         
#>  [16] "arg_max"          "arg_min"          "arg_sort"        
#>  [19] "arg_unique"       "argsort"          "backward_fill"   
#>  [22] "cast"             "ceil"             "clip"            
#>  [25] "clip_max"         "clip_min"         "cos"             
#>  [28] "cosh"             "count"            "cumcount"        
#>  [31] "cummax"           "cummin"           "cumprod"         
#>  [34] "cumsum"           "diff"             "div"             
#>  [37] "dot"              "drop_nans"        "drop_nulls"      
#>  [40] "eq"               "ewm_mean"         "ewm_std"         
#>  [43] "ewm_var"          "exclude"          "exp"             
#>  [46] "explode"          "extend_constant"  "extend_expr"     
#>  [49] "fill_nan"         "fill_null"        "filter"          
#>  [52] "first"            "flatten"          "floor"           
#>  [55] "forward_fill"     "gt"               "gt_eq"           
#>  [58] "hash"             "head"             "inspect"         
#>  [61] "interpolate"      "is_between"       "is_duplicated"   
#>  [64] "is_finite"        "is_first"         "is_in"           
#>  [67] "is_infinite"      "is_nan"           "is_not"          
#>  [70] "is_not_nan"       "is_not_null"      "is_null"         
#>  [73] "is_unique"        "keep_name"        "kurtosis"        
#>  [76] "last"             "len"              "limit"           
#>  [79] "list"             "lit"              "log"             
#>  [82] "log10"            "lower_bound"      "lt"              
#>  [85] "lt_eq"            "map"              "map_alias"       
#>  [88] "max"              "mean"             "median"          
#>  [91] "min"              "mode"             "mul"             
#>  [94] "n_unique"         "nan_max"          "nan_min"         
#>  [97] "neq"              "null_count"       "or"              
#> [100] "over"             "pct_change"       "pow"             
#> [103] "prefix"           "print"            "product"         
#> [106] "quantile"         "rank"             "rechunk"         
#> [109] "reinterpret"      "rep"              "rep_extend"      
#> [112] "repeat_by"        "reshape"          "reverse"         
#> [115] "rolling_max"      "rolling_mean"     "rolling_median"  
#> [118] "rolling_min"      "rolling_quantile" "rolling_skew"    
#> [121] "rolling_std"      "rolling_sum"      "rolling_var"     
#> [124] "round"            "rpow"             "sample"          
#> [127] "search_sorted"    "shift"            "shift_and_fill"  
#> [130] "shuffle"          "sign"             "sin"             
#> [133] "sinh"             "skew"             "slice"           
#> [136] "sort"             "sort_by"          "sqrt"            
#> [139] "std"              "sub"              "suffix"          
#> [142] "sum"              "tail"             "take"            
#> [145] "take_every"       "tan"              "tanh"            
#> [148] "to_physical"      "to_r"             "top_k"           
#> [151] "unique"           "upper_bound"      "value_counts"    
#> [154] "var"              "where"            "xor"             

pl$col("this_column")$sum()$over("that_column")
#> polars Expr: col("this_column").sum().over([col("that_column")])
#scalars to literal, explit `pl$lit(42)` implicit `+ 2`
pl$col("some_column") / pl$lit(42) + 2
#> polars Expr: [([(col("some_column")) / (42f64)]) + (2f64)]

#vector to literal explicitly via Series and back again
pl$DataFrame(list())$select(pl$lit(pl$Series(1:4)))$to_list()[[1L]] #R vector to expression and back again
#> [1] 1 2 3 4

#vectors to literal implicitly
(pl$lit(2) + 1:4 ) / 4:1
#> polars Expr: [([(2f64) + (Series)]) / (Series)]
pl$col("some")$suffix("_column")
#> polars Expr: RENAME_ALIAS col("some")
pl$col("some")$suffix("_column")
#> polars Expr: RENAME_ALIAS col("some")
pl$DataFrame(list(a=1:5))$select(pl$col("a")$reverse())
#> polars DataFrame: shape: (5, 1)
#> ┌─────┐
#> │ a   │
#> │ --- │
#> │ i32 │
#> ╞═════╡
#> │ 5   │
#> ├╌╌╌╌╌┤
#> │ 4   │
#> ├╌╌╌╌╌┤
#> │ 3   │
#> ├╌╌╌╌╌┤
#> │ 2   │
#> ├╌╌╌╌╌┤
#> │ 1   │
#> └─────┘
